vulnerabilities.description: The 'id' parameter is vulnerable to classic SQL injection. Attacker can dump entire database.
vulnerabilities.evidence: MySQL error: You have an error in your SQL syntax...
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements. Never concatenate user input directly into SQL.

vulnerabilities.description: Blind SQL injection detected. Server responds differently based on true/false conditions.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Response time difference detected (time-based blind SQLi)
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Use ORM frameworks like SQLAlchemy. Apply input validation and whitelist acceptable values.

vulnerabilities.description: Reflected XSS found. User-supplied input is returned in response without sanitization.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Script tag reflected in response body without encoding
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Encode all output using htmlspecialchars(). Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers.

vulnerabilities.description: Stored XSS found in the guestbook. Malicious script is saved and executed for every visitor.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Payload stored in database and rendered on page load
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Sanitize input on both client and server side. Use libraries like DOMPurify for output sanitization.

vulnerabilities.description: No account lockout mechanism. Application allows unlimited login attempts enabling brute force attacks.
vulnerabilities.evidence: 200 OK response with default credentials admin/password
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Implement account lockout after 5 failed attempts. Enforce strong password policy. Enable MFA.

vulnerabilities.description: Session cookie missing Secure and HttpOnly flags. Susceptible to session hijacking over HTTP.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Set Secure and HttpOnly flags on all session cookies. Use HTTPS exclusively.

vulnerabilities.description: Direct object reference allows access to any user profile by changing the user_id parameter.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Changing user_id returns different user's personal data without authorization check
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Implement proper authorization checks. Use indirect references. Apply RBAC/ABAC.

vulnerabilities.description: Vertical privilege escalation possible. Low-privilege user can edit admin records.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Record updated successfully without privilege check
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Validate user role before every sensitive operation. Never trust client-supplied IDs.

vulnerabilities.description: phpinfo() page exposed publicly. Reveals server configuration, PHP version, installed modules.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Full PHP environment info returned to unauthenticated users
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Remove phpinfo() files from production. Disable debug/diagnostic pages.

vulnerabilities.description: Local File Inclusion (LFI) vulnerability. Attacker can read arbitrary server files.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Contents of /etc/passwd returned in HTTP response
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Whitelist allowed file names. Never pass user-supplied filenames to filesystem functions.

vulnerabilities.description: DOM-based XSS via URL fragment. JavaScript reads location.hash and writes to DOM unsafely.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Payload executed in browser console without server round-trip
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Use safe DOM manipulation methods like textContent. Avoid innerHTML with user data.

vulnerabilities.description: CAPTCHA bypass possible by manipulating the 'step' parameter to skip verification.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Password change accepted without CAPTCHA validation
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Validate CAPTCHA server-side on every step. Do not rely on client-side state.
